You need JavaScript enabled to view it. Detailed Profile About Faculty: He is a faculty member here in the subject domains of industrial and production engineering, product design, and business/product analytics. He got awarded his doctorate of philosophy (PhD) degree in the research domain of 'design and development of new automotive model variants (NAMVs)' specifically related to the broad subject areas of 'New Product Design' and 'Product Analytics' from the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kharagpur. He has his PG degree: Master of Engineering (ME) in the discipline of ‘Industrial and Production Engineering (IPE)’ with the specialization in Industrial Engineering and Management (IEM) from Shri Govindram Seksaria Institute of Technology and Science (SGSITS) Indore scoring 83.10% (First Division with Honours) and UG degree: Bachelor of Engineering (BE) in the core discipline of Mechanical Engineering from the University Institute of Technology (UIT) Bhopal scoring 75.50% (First Division with Honours). He taught in government and private academic institutions both on a regular basis and as a visiting faculty member also and worked as a TA instructor (at IIT Kharagpur) in the academic courses related to the disciplines of industrial engineering and operations management, manufacturing science, mechanical engineering, management science, and business analytics curriculums. He has an academic experience of nearly 3.5 years as a teaching experience and research and academic experience (along with working on NPTEL project) of 7 years 11 months at IIT Kharagpur with an overall regular experience of about 12 years. Apart from this, he was a visiting faculty at the National Institute of Technical Teachers' Training and Research (NITTTR) Bhopal to teach one of the courses – 'Supply Chain Management' at PG Level in the ME, Advanced Production Systems (APS) curriculum on a short-term basis. He was also an adjunct faculty at Centre for Research in Materials Management (CRIMM), Indian Institute of Materials Management (IIMM), Kolkata where he was assigned supervision of Fellow theses as a 'Junior Research Guide' for the 'Fellowship Program in Materials Management'. His research areas of interest are new product design, product analytics, mathematical modelling and design of supply chain contracts, coordination policies and advanced inventory systems, world-class manufacturing (WCM), design for industrial safety systems (SLC Model, IEC 61508 Safety Standard, SIS, HAZOP, HIRA), and safety analytics. He is an active professional member of European Operations Management Association (EurOMA), India Chapter of Production and Operations Management Society (POMS), Indian Institution of Industrial Engineering (IIIE: SM), Quality Circle Forum of India (QCFI: LM), and Indian Society of Technical Education (ISTE: LM). He actively participated and contributed as a member of these renowned professional societies. He got awarded with MHRD, Govt. of India, Institute Fellowship in the year 2013 (autumn session) towards the fulfilment of his degree of ‘Doctorate of Philosophy (PhD)’ at Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kharagpur. He had been also nominated as the jury member at 29th, 30th, and 31st Chapter Conventions on Quality Concepts – CCQC 2016, CCQC 2017 and CCQC 2018 consecutively organized for the industry-based resource persons by Quality Circle Forum of India (QCFI), Kolkata Chapter at Taj Bengal, Kolkata, as one of his professional accomplishments. His other professional accomplishments are getting full-funded institute grants by the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kharagpur to internationally attend two of the best listed academic international conferences in his research domain – one of these, “Production and Operations Management Society (POMS) 28th Annual Meeting (POMS - 2017)” at Seattle, Washington State (WA; US), and another one, “25th Annual European Operations Management Association (EurOMA 2018) Conference” at Budapest University of Technology and Economics, Budapest (Hungary). Education and Qualification: No. Degree Year University/Institute Division Specialization 1. Ph. D. 2020 Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Kharagpur NA New Product Design (NPD) and Product Analytics 2. M.E. 2012 Shri Govindram Seksaria Institute of Technology and Science (SGSITS) Indore First (with Honours) Industrial Engineering and Management (IEM) 3. B.E. 2007 University Institute of Technology (UIT) Bhopal First (with Honours) Mechanical Engineering Work Experience: S. No. Designation Department Employer Duration 1 Assistant Professor Industrial & Production Engg. SGSITS, Indore From 27-08-2021 Till Date 2 Adjunct Faculty Centre for Research in Materials Management (CRIMM) IIMM Kolkata From 16-08-2018 to 10-06-2019 4 Assistant Professor Industrial & Production Engg. SGSITS, Indore 09-01-2013 to 31-05-2013 5. Assistant Professor Dept. of Mechanical Engineering TIT-C Bhopal (26-07-2012 to 08-01-2013) 6. Lecturer Dept. of Mechanical Engineering ASCT Bhopal (25-07-2008 to 24-07-2010) Research Details: Application Areas of New Product Innovativeness (NPI) and New Product Design/Development (NPD) Research Design and Development of New Automotive Model Variants (NAMVs) –Critical Capabilities and NPD Success Measures influencing the NPD process of NAMVs in the Automotive Industry Product Analytics: Application of Analytics and Statistical Tools and Techniques – R-programming Software, Structural Equation Modeling (SEM) using AMOS 24 Version Software and CRAN-R Package of Simulated SEM (SIMSEM) and IBM SPSS Statistics 22 Version in NPD Research Industry 4.0 and Internet of Things (IoT) Best Practices in Smart Manufacturing and Smart Product Development Mathematical Modelling, Formulation and Design of Supply Chain Contracts and Other Coordination Policies between the Supply Chain Partners Mathematical Modelling and Formulation of Special Cases in Inventory Systems/Models (e.g.; Models based on Inventory-dependent Demand) in the Supply Chain Network Implementation Aspects of Advanced Techniques in Industrial Safety – Safety Life Cycle (SLC) Model, IEC 61508 Safety Standard, Hazard and Operability Analysis (HAZOP), Hazard Identification and Risk Assessment (HIRA), and Safety Analytics Best Practices in World-class/Lean Manufacturing (WCM/LM) – KANBAN System, Single Minute Exchange of Die (SMED), Total Productive Maintenance (TPM), and Value Stream Mapping (VSM) and Value Stream Analysis (VSA) PhD Supervision: PhD under Supervision : 01 Name of the PhD Candidate: Ms. Rita Nagwal Research Domain: Circular Supply Chain integrated with Industry 4.0 Technologies/Applications Title of the PhD Thesis: “ Performance Improvement of Circular Supply Chain employing Industry 4.0 Applications for SMEs ” Current Status: PhD Thesis Submitted Fellow Thesis supervised as an Adjunct Faculty (Junior Research Guide) in ‘Fellowship Program in Materials Management’ of Centre for Research in Materials Management (CRIMM), Indian Institute of Materials Management (IIMM), Kolkata : 01 Name of the Fellow Candidate: Mr Pachamuthu Sengottaiyan Research Domain: Lean Manufacturing, Supply Chain Management Title of the Fellow Thesis: “Enabling Indian MSME’s to face the Global Challenges - A Research based Road Map” Current Status: Completed Publications: Technical Papers’ Publications International/National Journals [1] Rohit, K. , Ghosh, K.K., Bhattacharya, S., and Pal, P., (2021), “Development of Integrated Framework to introduce New Automotive Model Variants: An Analytic Approach”, Technovation (SCI and Web of Science Indexed; H-Index: 121) [Under 2 nd Phase of Review] [2] Rohit, K. , and Ghosh, K.K., , “Mathematical Modelling and Formulation of Buyback Supply Chain Contract under the Impact of Inventory-dependent Demand Rate Function: A Profit-sharing Mechanism for Supply Chain Partners”, Cogent Engineering (ESCI and Web of Science Indexed; H-Index: 24) [Accepted/In-Press] [3] Rohit, K. , Ghosh, K.K., and Bhattacharya, S., (2018), “Significance of Industrial Engineering Competencies to Introduce New Automotive Variants: A Study Perspective”, Industrial Engineering Journal, XI (2), pp.14-19 [4] Kanungo, B., Rohit, K. , and Thakar, G., (2017), “Unknowingly Accepting Health Hazards in Daily Routine Life – A Survey in a Small Town of Madhya Pradesh”, Industrial Engineering Journal, X (6), pp.29-33 [5] Rohit, K. , Bhattacharya, S., and Guin, K.K., (2016), “Assessing the Stock-Dependency Attribute of Demand Rate Function and Its Applicability: A Study Perspective”, Industrial Engineering Journal, IX (7), pp.06-10 [6] Rohit, K. , Gupta, R.C., Thakar, G.D., and Mahajan, M.D., (2014), “Assessment of Safety Life Cycle (SLC) Model to Overcome the Safety Hazards: A Case Study”, Industrial Engineering Journal, VII (4), pp.20-24 [7] Rathaur, G., Rohit, K. , Dandekar, M.D., and Thakar, G.D., (2014), “Performance Evaluation of Value Stream Analysis (VSA): Case Study of a Manufacturing Unit”, Industrial Engineering Journal, VII (10), pp.18-22 Book Chapters [1] Rohit, K. , Verma, P.L., and Ghosh, K.K., (2018), “Assessment of Significance of Safety Life Cycle Management (SLCM) Approach using IEC 61508 Safety Standard towards its Implementation in the Foundry Shop: Case Study of a Pump Manufacturing Industry”, “ Industrial Safety Management : 21st Century Perspectives of Asia; Managing the Asian Century (Springer Series) , Springer Nature - Scientific Publishing Services (P) Ltd., Singapore, pp.129-141, Chap.10 (eBook – ISBN: 978-981-10-6328-2; electronic – ISSN: 2364-5865) [2] Rohit, K. , Ghosh, K.K., Gupta, R.C., and Bhattacharya, S., (2019), “Prioritization of Critical Attributes to introduce New Automotive Model Variants: A Sustainable Model Framework for Technological Innovation”, “ Proceedings of the International Conference on Technology Innovation and Data Sciences (ICTIDS 2019) ” (Accepted for Publication as a Book Chapter in Springer Series), Springer Nature - Scientific Publishing Services Pvt. Ltd. [3] Rohit, K. , Rathaur, G., and Dalpati, A., (2012), “Effectiveness of the Seven Value Stream Mapping (VSM) Tools towards Lean Improvement: Case Study of a Manufacturing Unit”, “ Advances in Operations and Supply Chain Management ”, Society of Operations Management (SOM), Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Delhi, New Delhi (India), Vol.1, pp. 450-458, Chap.42 (Manufacturing Systems, Strategies and Management) [4] Rohit, K. , Mahajan, M.D., and Kanungo, B., (2012), “Implication of Safety Life Cycle Management (SLCM) Approach using IEC 61508 Safety Standard in an Engineering Industry”, “ Business Innovations and Entrepreneurship – Transforming World Economy ”, Excel Books, New Delhi (India), pp.443-450, Chap.50 (ISBN: 978-93-5062-004-5) [5] Rohit, K. , (2011), “Case-Analysis on eCRM in Online Banking”, “ Management Perspectives –Emerging Trends and Leadership Challenges ”, Popular Book Depot, Jaipur (India), Vol.1, pp.355-366, Chap.54 (Section – Information Technology) (ISBN: 978-81-86098-06-6) Technical Papers – International /National Conferences [1] Rohit, K. , Ghosh, K.K., and Bhattacharya, S., (2018), “Development of a conceptual framework to introduce new automotive variants: Insights from new product development research in automotive domain”, “ Proceedings of the 25 th Annual European Operations Management Association (EurOMA 2018) Conference ”, Budapest (Hungary) [2] Rohit, K. , Bhattacharya, S., and Ghosh, K.K., (2018), “Designing the supplier-based revenue sharing contract for the goods experiencing inventory-dependent demand: A mathematical framework”, “ Proceedings of the 25 th Annual European Operations Management Association (EurOMA 2018) Conference ”, Budapest (Hungary) [3] Rohit, K. , Ghosh, K.K., Bhattacharya, S., and Gupta, R.C., (2017), “Study of Best Operations Management Practices as Key Organizational Competencies to introduce New Automotive Variants: Insights from the New Product Development Research in Automotive Domain ”, “ Proceedings of the XXI Annual International Conference of the Society of Operations Management (SOM 2017) ”, Ahmedabad University, Ahmedabad (India) [4] Rohit, K. , Bhattacharya, S., Guin, K.K. and Ghosh, K.K., (2017), “Design of a Mathematical Framework for Supplier-Based Revenue Sharing Coordination Policy under Inventory-dependent Demand Rate”, “ Proceedings of the Production and Operations Management Society (POMS) 28 th Annual Meeting (POMS - 2017) ”, Bellevue-Seattle, Washington State (WA; USA) [5] Rathaur, G., Rohit, K. , and Dalpati, A., (2017), “Assessing the Effectiveness of Value Stream Analysis towards the Lean Improvements: An Application-Based Approach”, “ Proceedings of the Production and Operations Management Society (POMS) 28 th Annual Meeting (POMS - 2017) ”, Bellevue-Seattle, Washington State (WA; USA) [6] Rohit, K. , Guin, K.K., Ghosh, K.K., and Bhattacharya, S., (2016), “Comparison of the Modelling Aspects of the Supplier-based Revenue Sharing Coordination Policy with the Non-Coordinated Case under the Impact of Stock-Dependent Demand Rate Function: A Review”, “ The Fifth Biennial Supply Chain Management Conference ”, Indian Institute of Management
